description: Building an HNSW index on a large table exceeds the memory budget and crashes the build.
body: Failure mode: HNSW graph construction holds the growing graph in memory, and on a large table with a high connectivity parameter it exceeds maintenance_work_mem (or the container limit) and the build OOMs, sometimes taking the instance with it. Detection signals: the build is killed by the OOM killer, memory usage climbs steadily during CREATE INDEX, and a smaller subset builds fine. Do not start an unbounded full-table HNSW build without checking the memory estimate. Safer fallback: validate the index size on a subset, raise the maintenance memory budget deliberately or lower the connectivity parameter, and build in a controlled window with monitoring.
